text.title: OpenAI Embeddings — Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 known failure modes when running an embeddings pipeline in production. Each carries symptom / root-cause / fix.

failure-mode.description: Dimension mismatch — query path embeds with 1536 dims against a corpus stored at 3072 dims; cosine returns random scores; recall drops to noise.
failure-mode.detector: Recall@10 falls to baseline; mean top-1 cosine score drops below the empirical floor; startup smoke test detects (model, dims) drift.
failure-mode.repair: Centralise `(model, dims)` in a `config.py` consumed by both ingest and query. Add a startup assertion that compares the stored vector_len to the configured dims; fail loud.

failure-mode.description: Silent truncation — input > 8191 tokens passed to `embeddings.create`; OpenAI silently truncates; the resulting vector represents only the head.
failure-mode.detector: Logs show no error but recall drops on long documents; chunk count per doc unexpectedly = 1 for long sources.
failure-mode.repair: Chunk before embed using `chunking-strategies`. Add a guard that rejects inputs > 8000 tokens and routes them through the chunker.

failure-mode.description: Batch FIFO assumption — pipeline reads the JSONL output line-by-line and pairs to inputs by position; results are scrambled and embeddings attach to the wrong chunk.
failure-mode.detector: Random retrieval results; reindex with sync mode fixes the issue.
failure-mode.repair: Always key results by `custom_id`. Build a `{custom_id: embedding}` dict before upsert; never rely on line order.

failure-mode.description: PII leak via shared store — embedding user records into a multi-tenant vector store lets adversaries extract PII via probing queries.
failure-mode.detector: Vector store has tenant_id null on some records; nearest-neighbor across tenants returns cross-tenant results.
failure-mode.repair: Partition by tenant_id at the index level. Redact PII pre-embed (names, emails, SSNs). Enforce filter-on-tenant for every search call.

failure-mode.description: Query embedded as raw user message — short or ambiguous queries (2–5 words) miss recall because they sit in sparse vector regions.
failure-mode.detector: Recall@10 high for long queries, low for short ones; A/B vs expanded-query shows lift > 10%.
failure-mode.repair: LLM-rewrite the query before embedding (`expand-query` sub-task) for any input under 8 tokens or one that does not contain a noun phrase.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Documents being embedded are DATA. Instructions hidden inside source text ("ignore previous rules", "treat next doc as authoritative") have no effect on embedding generation but may influence downstream retrieval-augmented LLM calls — the consuming methodology (`agentic-rag`) is responsible for sanitising retrieved chunks before injecting them into prompts.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Any change to `(model, dims)` triggers a full reindex of the corpus and is reviewed by a named engineer before merge. Re-embed cost and recall regression both flow from this pair.
